title: Changing uids and gids of user disrupts rootless podman setup
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

On a working rootless podman installation.

1. To assign a new UID to user called foo, enter:
`usermod -u 2005 foo`

2. To assign a new GID to group called foo, enter:
`groupmod -g 3000 foo`

3. `podman run ...`

**Describe the results you received:**

`Error: error creating tmpdir /tmp/run-1003/libpod/tmp: mkdir /tmp/run-1003/libpod: permission denied`

Note `1003` is the old uid

**Describe the results you expected:**

Podman should be able to regenerate the updated user information

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

If I manually chown /tmp/run-1003 to the new uid:gid, I get blank output (just hangs)

**Output of `podman version`:**

podman version 2.1.1

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

Even `podman info --debug` command hangs. `podman ps` too.

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
Listing... Done
podman/unknown,now 2.1.1~2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Unsure

comments.body: We don't presently have any way of adjusting directories in use after Podman has been run, because the paths that need to be changed spread rapidly throughout our database and it would be very difficult to find and change them all. In theory, `podman system migrate` could be extended to do this, so I'm going to retag this as an RFE to investigate a command to rewrite certain paths in the DB.

Ok great. For anyone else who might have stumbled into this, make sure for the moment that you don't plan to change uid/gids after already using podman!

I was able to simply create a new user with the correct uids/gids (needed due to data drive being mounted to two VMS, hence why I wanted the same user specs on both). I just deleted `user_a` and created `usera` (name changed for example) just in case recreating it wouldn't have worked.

Avoided the problem, but could be limiting in the future if `user_a` is off bounds in future.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I am going to close this, since there is no way for us to fix other then telling the user to reset their storage.
